name: action
useFor: Triggers something on click
avoidFor: Button, SplitButton

name: input
useFor: Captures user data
avoidFor: Input, Textarea, Select, Combobox, Checkbox, Switch, Slider

name: selection
useFor: Pick from a small fixed set
avoidFor: Tabs, SegmentControl, RadioGroup, RadioTile, ToggleGroup

name: menu
useFor: Floating list of actions/options
avoidFor: DropdownMenu, ContextMenu, Menubar

name: overlay
useFor: Floats over the page
avoidFor: Dialog, AlertDialog, Drawer, Popover, HoverCard, Tooltip, Sonner

name: feedback
useFor: Communicates system state
avoidFor: Alert, Badge, Status, Spinner, Skeleton, Progress, Empty

name: display
useFor: Presents data
avoidFor: Table, DataTree, Card, KeyValuePair, Avatar, Tag, Item, Chart, Kbd

name: navigation
useFor: Moves the user across views
avoidFor: Navbar, Breadcrumb, Pagination, EditorTabs

name: layout
useFor: Structures the surface
avoidFor: Accordion, Collapsible, Resizable, ScrollArea, Separator, AspectRatio, Direction, Field, Label, InputGroup

name: chrome
useFor: Page/app-level scaffolding
avoidFor: PageHeader, PlatformHeader

name: Button
useFor: Any click action — primary CTAs, toolbar actions, icon buttons, menu triggers
avoidFor: State that persists across clicks → use `Toggle`; on/off settings → `Switch`

name: SplitButton
useFor: A primary action with related alternatives in a dropdown (3–5 alternatives)
avoidFor: Unrelated actions → split into separate `Button`s; >5 actions → use `DropdownMenu`

name: Toggle
useFor: Buttons that need to remember pressed state (icon toolbar toggles, filter pills)
avoidFor: Stateless actions → use `Button`; on/off settings → `Switch`

name: Checkbox
useFor: Multi-select within a group, indeterminate parent states, form fields with submit
avoidFor: On/off settings that take effect immediately → use `Switch`

name: Combobox
useFor: Pick one or many from 10+ options with search
avoidFor: Fewer than 10 fixed options → use `Select`

name: Input
useFor: Single-line text fields, numbers, search, email — md size by default
avoidFor: Multi-line → use `Textarea`; >10 options → `Combobox`; search inside a control → `InputGroup`

name: NativeSelect
useFor: Native HTML `<select>` for accessibility-first or environments without JS
avoidFor: Anything else — prefer `Select` for visual consistency

name: Select
useFor: Pick one from ≤10 fixed options
avoidFor: More than 10 options or needs search → use `Combobox`

name: Slider
useFor: Continuous range selection where the rough value matters more than the exact one
avoidFor: Exact number entry → pair with `Input`; discrete steps → use `SegmentControl` or `RadioGroup`

name: Switch
useFor: Immediate on/off toggles that take effect without a save action
avoidFor: Inside forms requiring submit → use `Checkbox`; multi-select → `Checkbox`

name: Textarea
useFor: Multi-line text input that auto-grows (`field-sizing: content`)
avoidFor: Single-line input → use `Input`; rich text → flag the gap

name: RadioGroup
useFor: One-of-many choice for 2–7 options with text labels
avoidFor: On/off → use `Switch`; visual tile choices → use `RadioTile`

name: RadioTile
useFor: Visual one-of-many with icon + title + description (2–5 options)
avoidFor: Plain text labels → use `RadioGroup`; >5 options → use `Select`

name: SegmentControl
useFor: Single-value selection in toolbars (2–5 single-word items)
avoidFor: Primary view switching → use `Tabs`; >5 items → `Select`

name: ToggleGroup
useFor: A row of `Toggle`s that act as a single-select or multi-select group
avoidFor: Single value → use `SegmentControl` or `RadioGroup`

name: ContextMenu
useFor: Right-click actions on a target
avoidFor: Left-click action menus → use `DropdownMenu`

name: DropdownMenu
useFor: Click-triggered action menus, kebab/overflow menus, item-level menus
avoidFor: Hover-only menus; lists with search → use `Combobox`

name: Menubar
useFor: App-level menu bar (File / Edit / View / …)
avoidFor: Single trigger menus → use `DropdownMenu`

name: AlertDialog
useFor: Confirming destructive or irreversible actions
avoidFor: Non-destructive confirmations → use `Dialog`

name: Dialog
useFor: Focused tasks and forms in an overlay (max one open at a time)
avoidFor: Destructive confirmations → use `AlertDialog`; secondary detail panels → use `Drawer`

name: Drawer
useFor: Detail panels, settings, secondary content sliding in from the right
avoidFor: Primary workflows → use `Dialog`; in-flow content → use `Card`

name: HoverCard
useFor: Rich preview content on hover (user profiles, link previews)
avoidFor: Anything interactive — HoverCard is read-only → use `Popover`

name: Popover
useFor: Date pickers, color pickers, mini-forms, rich tooltips with content
avoidFor: Plain text → use `Tooltip`; complex content → use `Dialog`

name: Sonner
useFor: Transient feedback toasts after an action (save, copy, delete)
avoidFor: Critical errors that need user action → use `Alert`; persistent state → `Badge`

name: Tooltip
useFor: One-line hover hints, especially on icon-only buttons (`size="icon-md"`)
avoidFor: Interactive content → use `Popover`; rich preview → `HoverCard`; >1 line of text → `Popover`

name: Alert
useFor: Persistent inline messages tied to page content (info / warning / danger / success)
avoidFor: Transient feedback → use `Sonner` (Toast)

name: Badge
useFor: Short status labels and counts (1–2 words)
avoidFor: Interactive triggers → use `Button`; status with icon → `Status`

name: Empty
useFor: Zero-data states with title, description, primary action
avoidFor: While loading → use `Skeleton`; recoverable error → use `Alert`

name: Progress
useFor: Determinate progress with a known endpoint (percentage or step count)
avoidFor: Indeterminate progress → use `Spinner`

name: Skeleton
useFor: Initial-load placeholders that match the shape of the incoming content
avoidFor: Loads longer than ~3 s → switch to `Spinner`; data-empty states → `Empty`

name: Spinner
useFor: Indeterminate loading >1 s
avoidFor: Initial page loads → use `Skeleton` first

name: Status
useFor: Status with icon + label (running, error, success, online, …)
avoidFor: Plain counts/labels → use `Badge`; interactive → use `Button`

name: Avatar
useFor: User identity (image, fallback initials, default icon)
avoidFor: Decorative purposes — Avatar implies a real user entity

name: Card
useFor: Bounded content surfaces with elevation (rounded-xl, shadow)
avoidFor: Full-width / unbounded content; nesting cards inside cards

name: Chart
useFor: Data visualization (recharts wrapper)
avoidFor: Static decoration — Chart implies real data

name: DataTree
useFor: Hierarchical data browsing (catalogs, schemas, tables) — every node has a typed icon
avoidFor: User-mutable file hierarchies → use file-tree variants of DataTree; flat lists → use `Table`

name: Item
useFor: Generic list-item slot used inside menus, navbars, and command lists
avoidFor: Custom one-off list rows — prefer purpose-specific components first

name: Kbd
useFor: Inline keyboard shortcut markup (`⌘K`, `Ctrl+P`)
avoidFor: Modifier indicators in body copy without keys → just text

name: KeyValuePair
useFor: Metadata in detail sidebars (key uses muted-foreground; value uses foreground)
avoidFor: Editable values → use `Field` + `Input`

name: Table
useFor: Structured data with sortable columns, row hover, optional row actions
avoidFor: Layout grids → use Tailwind grid/flex; hierarchical data → `DataTree`

name: Tag
useFor: Removable filter chips, key:value labels in faceted filters
avoidFor: Read-only status → use `Badge`; persistent labels with no remove → `Badge`

name: Breadcrumb
useFor: Hierarchical path back to root (last item is current page)
avoidFor: Non-hierarchical navigation → use `Tabs` or `Navbar`

name: EditorTabs
useFor: Code-editor style closeable tabs with file-type icons
avoidFor: General view switching → use `Tabs`

name: Navbar
useFor: Top-level product navigation (workspace switcher, global actions)
avoidFor: Page-level tabs → use `Tabs`; in-page sections → use `Accordion`

name: Pagination
useFor: Paged tables with previous/next, ellipsis for large counts
avoidFor: Fewer than 2 pages; infinite scroll

name: Tabs
useFor: Switch between sibling content panels of equal importance (2–7 tabs)
avoidFor: Unrelated pages → use `Navbar`; closeable per-file → `EditorTabs`; toolbar selection → `SegmentControl`

name: Accordion
useFor: Settings panels, filter groups, FAQ-style content
avoidFor: Primary navigation → use `Tabs`. Don't nest accordions.

name: AspectRatio
useFor: Lock image/video proportions inside cards and grids
avoidFor: Text-only containers — apply only to media wrappers

name: Collapsible
useFor: Single show/hide toggle (one expandable region)
avoidFor: Multiple sibling regions → use `Accordion`

name: Direction
useFor: RTL/LTR direction wrapping
avoidFor: (utility — wrap as needed)

name: Field
useFor: Form field wrapper that pairs Label + control + helper/error text
avoidFor: Free-floating inputs without labels — Inputs should always have a Label

name: InputGroup
useFor: Attach icons, buttons, or text addons to an `Input` (e.g. search + clear, prefix/suffix)
avoidFor: Standalone inputs without addons → use `Input` directly

name: Label
useFor: Form labels (Bold style, 13px Semibold)
avoidFor: Sub-labels or helper text — use a span with `text-muted-foreground` instead

name: Resizable
useFor: Resizable split panels (e.g. tree rail + detail)
avoidFor: Fixed-width panels — only use when the user should resize

name: ScrollArea
useFor: Overflowing content that needs custom-styled scrollbars
avoidFor: Page-level scroll — let the browser handle it

name: Separator
useFor: Visual divider between content groups
avoidFor: Decorative use — only when grouping is real

name: PageHeader
useFor: Top of every content surface — breadcrumb, title row, tabs
avoidFor: Inside cards or sub-sections — only one PageHeader per surface

name: PlatformHeader
useFor: The Databricks 48px top bar (sidebar toggle, search, workspace, Genie, apps, profile)
avoidFor: Custom top bars — every product page uses PlatformHeader via `Base` shell

name: ButtonGroup
useFor: Use `SplitButton`. File kept for back-compat only.
